title: cicuta douglasii var. occidentalis
intro: Cicuta douglasii var. occidentalis, also known as western water hemlock, is a highly toxic plant species that belongs to the carrot family. Historically, the plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. However, due to its toxicity, it is essential to approach its use with caution and under the guidance of a qualified practitioner. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in small amounts and in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health benefits, such as relieving pain and reducing inflammation.
